title: M_A_Y_09 [DONTTAKEITSERIOUS,IMALITTLEKID]
body:
# M_A_Y_09 [DONTTAKEITSERIOUS,IMALITTLEKID]

**Project:** [[projects/alphabets]]  
**Period:** May 2009  
**Format:** M4A  
**Status:** Archive-only  
**Subtitle:** "DON'T TAKE IT SERIOUS, I'M A LITTLE KID"  

## Tracklist (from archive filenames)

1. maygrading and vocal smash
2. may roar roar
3. maybe seal or otter
4. maypears
5. may rays
6. maybreazy
7. melee: rough seas angler fish lantern fights
8. mayday
9. may lions dumb club party ballad anthem [ahyah oh mm ey]
10. maybird with tummy ache
11. maybe bigfoot sings love songs in the deep night
12. maywolves hum and talk w/ shooting stars in the background
13. (track 13 missing)
14. may world dream love hope child

13 confirmed tracks (track 13 absent from archive, track 14 confirmed).

## Context

The May 2009 album fills another gap in the album-a-month series. This folder's full title — "DON'T TAKE IT SERIOUS, I'M A LITTLE KID" — is a direct autobiographical statement from Colin at 18–19 years old, both a disavowal of seriousness and an ownership of youth. It's one of the most characterful folder titles in the archive.

The May-naming convention for tracks ("maygrading," "may roar roar," "maypears," "maybreazy," "maywolves") is consistent with the seasonal/month-themed titling seen in other releases of this period. The track "mayday" may be a play on both the month and the distress call.

ASD files confirm Ableton Live was in use for at least "maybe bigfoot sings love songs in the deep night."

## Sources

- Archive folder: `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/M_A_Y_09[DONTTAKEITSERIOUS,IMALITTLEKID]/` (13+ M4A files)
